notam.notam_html:
ENGINE ICE SHEDDING: PILOTS ARE REQUESTED TO REPORT NECESSITY FOR
ICE SHEDDING TO ATC PRIOR REQUEST FOR TAXI.
TO AVOID RWY CAPACITY REDUCTION ICE SHEDDING WITH TWIN AND TRIPLE
ENGINE A/C SHOULD BE PERFORMED ON AN ASSIGNED TWY LOCATION WHENEVER
POSSIBLE (MAX. 70 PERCENT THRUST). EXPECT ASSIGNMENT OF AN AREA ON A
STRAIGHT PORTION OF A TWY DEPENDENT TO THE DEPARTURE RWY.
IF ICE SHEDDING MUST BE PERFORMED ON THE RWY: REPORT TO ATC PRIOR
LINEUP IF ROLLING T/O CANNOT BE PERFORMED OR T/O ROLL WILL BE
DELAYED BY MORE THAN 10 SECONDS AFTER T/O CLEARANCE.

notam.notam_html:
TAXI PROCEDURES FOR ARRIVING AIRCRAFT READ AS FOLLOWS:
ATC EXPECTS ARRIVING ACFT TO VACATE RUNWAYS VIA THE RAPID EXIT
TAXIWAYS. IF UNABLE TO DO SO, NOTIFY BUDAPEST APPROACH ON FIRST
CONTACT. ARRIVALS ON RWY 13R TO T1, USE TWY B1 OR A1. RESTRICTIONS
ON RAPID EXIT TAXIWAYS J4, Y AND Z WILL BE PROVIDED BY BUDAPEST
TOWER WITH LANDING CLEARANCE. DURING LOW VISIBILITY OPERATIONS,
PILOTS SHALL REPORT RWY VACATION TO BUDAPEST TOWER ON 118.715CH. THE
BACKTRACK (180-DEGREE TURN) MANOEUVRES ON RUNWAYS WITH AIRCRAFT
WINGSPAN AT OR HIGHER THAN 36M IS NOT ALLOWED DUE TO WIDTH OF
RUNWAY.
AFTER VACATING THE RWY, WITHOUT FURTHER NOTICE, PILOTS SHALL
IMMEDIATELY CONTACT BUDAPEST GROUND ON 121.905CH FOR DETAILED TAXI
INSTRUCTIONS, IF NOT OTHERWISE INSTRUCTED BY ATC. FURTHER TAXIING TO
THE DESIGNATED STAND IS ONLY ALLOWED WHEN CLEARED BY BUDAPEST GROUND
OR BUDAPEST TOWER.
REF AIP LHBP AD 2.20 3.3.

ATC EXPECTS ARRIVING ACFT TO VACATE RUNWAYS VIA THE RAPID EXIT 
TAXIWAYS. IF UNABLE TO DO SO, NOTIFY BUDAPEST APPROACH ON FIRST 
CONTACT. ARRIVALS ON RWY 13R TO T1, USE TWY B1 OR A1. RESTRICTIONS 
ON RAPID EXIT TAXIWAYS J4, Y AND Z WILL BE PROVIDED BY BUDAPEST 
TOWER WITH LANDING CLEARANCE. DURING LOW VISIBILITY OPERATIONS, 
PILOTS SHALL REPORT RWY VACATION TO BUDAPEST TOWER ON 118.715CH.
THE BACKTRACK (180-DEGREE TURN) MANOEUVRES ON RUNWAYS WITH AIRCRAFT 
WINGSPAN AT OR HIGHER THAN 36M IS NOT ALLOWED DUE TO WIDTH OF 
RUNWAY.
AFTER VACATING THE RWY, WITHOUT FURTHER NOTICE, PILOTS SHALL 
IMMEDIATELY CONTACT BUDAPEST GROUND ON 121.905CH FOR DETAILED TAXI 
INSTRUCTIONS, IF NOT OTHERWISE INSTRUCTED BY ATC. FURTHER TAXIING TO 
THE DESIGNATED STAND IS ONLY ALLOWED WHEN CLEARED BY BUDAPEST GROUND 
OR BUDAPEST TOWER.
REF AIP LHBP AD 2.20 3.3.

A0300/26 NOTAMN
Q) LOVV/QAFXX/IV/NBO/E /000/999/4743N01319E159
A) LOVV PART 1 OF 2 B) 2602161540 C) 2605151500 EST
E) IN ACCORDANCE WITH ARTICLE 3D OF REGULATION (EU) NO 833/2014, THE 
GENERAL PRINCIPLE IS THAT A FLIGHT IS PROHIBITED IF:
1. THE AIRCRAFT IS OPERATED BY A RUSSIAN AIR CARRIER,
2. THE AIRCRAFT IS REGISTERED IN RUSSIA,
3. THE AIRCRAFT IS OWNED OR CHARTERED OR OTHERWISE CONTROLLED BY ANY 
RUSSIAN NATURAL OR LEGAL PERSON, ENTITY OR BODY, OR
4. THE AIRCRAFT IS USED FOR A NON-SCHEDULED FLIGHT WITH REGARD TO 
WHICH A RUSSIAN NATURAL OR LEGAL PERSON, ENTITY OR BODY IS IN A 
POSITION TO EFFECTIVELY DETERMINE THE PLACE OR TIME OF ITS TAKE-OFF 
OR LANDING, 

EXCEPTIONS MAY BE REQUESTED BY CONTACTING
FLIGHTPERMISSION(AT)AUSTROCONTROL.AT FOR THE PURPOSE OF EMERGENCY 
FLIGHTS OR HUMANITARIAN FLIGHTS.

AIRCRAFT OPERATORS SHALL PROVIDE FOR NON-SCHEDULED FLIGHTS THE 
FOLLOWING INFORMATION AT THE REQUEST OF AUSTRO CONTROL GMBH, IN 
ADVANCE OF LANDING IN, TAKING OFF FROM OR OVERFLYING THE TERRITORY 
OF THE UNION: 
 
1. CREDIBLE AND SATISFACTORY INFORMATION REGARDING THE ACTUAL 
ULTIMATE BENEFICIAL OWNER OF THE AIRCRAFT AND, WHERE APPLICABLE, OF 
THE NATURAL OR LEGAL PERSON, ENTITY OR BODY ULTIMATELY CHARTERING 
THE AIRCRAFT, AND
A0300/26 NOTAMN
Q) LOVV/QAFXX/IV/NBO/E /000/999/4743N01319E159
A) LOVV PART 2 OF 2 B) 2602161540 C) 2605151500 EST
E) 2. A GENERAL DECLARATION, PASSENGER MANIFEST AND OTHER OFFICIAL 
DOCUMENTS DISPLAYING THE FULL NAMES, BIRTH DATES, BIRTH PLACES AND 
NATIONALITIES OF ALL PASSENGERS AND THE CREW MEMBERS, WHERE 
REASONABLE GROUNDS TO SUSPECT CIRCUMVENTION EXIST, BASED ON FACTORS 
SUCH AS ROUTING AND ORIGIN OF THE FLIGHT, OR INFORMATION ABOUT THE 
RELEVANT OPERATOR.
 
AIRCRAFT OPERATORS OF NON-SCHEDULED FLIGHTS BETWEEN RUSSIA AND THE 
UNION, OPERATED DIRECTLY OR VIA A THIRD COUNTRY, SHALL NOTIFY PRIOR 
TO THEIR OPERATION, AND AT LEAST 48 HOURS IN ADVANCE, ALL RELEVANT 
INFORMATION CONCERNING THE FLIGHT TO 
FLIGHTPERMISSION(AT)AUSTROCONTROL.AT

A0300/26   
  IN ACCORDANCE WITH ARTICLE 3D OF REGULATION (EU) NO 833/2014, THE 
  GENERAL PRINCIPLE IS THAT A FLIGHT IS PROHIBITED IF:
  1. THE AIRCRAFT IS OPERATED BY A RUSSIAN AIR CARRIER,
  2. THE AIRCRAFT IS REGISTERED IN RUSSIA,
  3. THE AIRCRAFT IS OWNED OR CHARTERED OR OTHERWISE CONTROLLED BY
  ANY RUSSIAN NATURAL OR LEGAL PERSON, ENTITY OR BODY, OR
  4. THE AIRCRAFT IS USED FOR A NON-SCHEDULED FLIGHT WITH REGARD TO
  WHICH A RUSSIAN NATURAL OR LEGAL PERSON, ENTITY OR BODY IS IN A
  POSITION TO EFFECTIVELY DETERMINE THE PLACE OR TIME OF ITS
  TAKE-OFF OR LANDING,
  EXCEPTIONS MAY BE REQUESTED BY CONTACTING
  FLIGHTPERMISSION(AT)AUSTROCONTROL.AT FOR THE PURPOSE OF EMERGENCY
  FLIGHTS OR HUMANITARIAN FLIGHTS.
  AIRCRAFT OPERATORS SHALL PROVIDE FOR NON-SCHEDULED FLIGHTS THE
  FOLLOWING INFORMATION AT THE REQUEST OF AUSTRO CONTROL GMBH, IN
  ADVANCE OF LANDING IN, TAKING OFF FROM OR OVERFLYING THE TERRITORY
  OF THE UNION:
  1. CREDIBLE AND SATISFACTORY INFORMATION REGARDING THE ACTUAL
  ULTIMATE BENEFICIAL OWNER OF THE AIRCRAFT AND, WHERE APPLICABLE,
  OF THE NATURAL OR LEGAL PERSON, ENTITY OR BODY ULTIMATELY
  CHARTERING THE AIRCRAFT, AND
  
  2. A GENERAL DECLARATION, PASSENGER MANIFEST AND OTHER OFFICIAL
  DOCUMENTS DISPLAYING THE FULL NAMES, BIRTH DATES, BIRTH PLACES AND
  NATIONALITIES OF ALL PASSENGERS AND THE CREW MEMBERS, WHERE
  REASONABLE GROUNDS TO SUSPECT CIRCUMVENTION EXIST, BASED ON
  FACTORS SUCH AS ROUTING AND ORIGIN OF THE FLIGHT, OR INFORMATION
  ABOUT THE RELEVANT OPERATOR.
  AIRCRAFT OPERATORS OF NON-SCHEDULED FLIGHTS BETWEEN RUSSIA AND THE
  UNION, OPERATED DIRECTLY OR VIA A THIRD COUNTRY, SHALL NOTIFY
  PRIOR TO THEIR OPERATION, AND AT LEAST 48 HOURS IN ADVANCE, ALL
  RELEVANT INFORMATION CONCERNING THE FLIGHT TO
  FLIGHTPERMISSION(AT)AUSTROCONTROL.AT

[ Company NOTAM ]
--------------------------------------------------------------------
==========
CREW ALERT
==========

SB007/14   
  SUBJECT: AUTO COST INDEXES
  WHEN PLANNING A COST INDEX, 2 OPTIONS ARE AVAILABLE. PILOTS MAY
  EITHER SELECT A SPECIFIC COST INDEX NUMBER FROM THE LIST, OR THEY
  MAY SELECT "AUTO". WHEN PLANNING AN "AUTO" COST INDEX, THE SYSTEM
  WILL REFERENCE THE SCHEDULED "TIME ENROUTE" OPTION AND ATTEMPT TO
  CHOOSE A COST INDEX WHICH CLOSELY MATCHES THIS VALUE. NOTE THAT
  THE "TIME ENROUTE" OPTION IS MEANT AS A GATE TO GATE TIME (AS
  INDICATED ON AN AIRLINE'S FLIGHT SCHEDULE, FOR EXAMPLE). IF A
  "TIME ENROUTE" OF 2:30 IS SELECTED, THE SYSTEM WILL SUBTRACT THE
  TAXI TIMES FROM THIS VALUE TO DETERMINE THE SCHEDULED AIR TIME. IT
  WILL THEN DETERMINE AND USE THE COST INDEX WHICH MATCHES THIS AIR
  TIME.

SB003/13   
  SUBJECT: FLIGHT RELEASE UNITS
  ALL FLIGHT CREW: PLEASE PAY SPECIAL ATTENTION TO THE UNITS
  SELECTION WHEN GENERATING A FLIGHT PLAN. FAILURE TO IDENTIFY THE
  CORRECT UNITS WHEN REFUELING PRIOR TO FLIGHT CAN RESULT IN
  DEPARTING WITH INSUFFICIENT FUEL AND/OR AN ERRONEOUS PAYLOAD.

=============
CREW BULLETIN
=============

NIL
